name: Pricing Usage
description: Usage-based pricing section with volume tiers, calculator, feature comparison table, and FAQ accordion.

standard.description: Full pricing section with tiers, calculator, and comparison

visual_brief: Usage-based pricing section with a monthly/annual toggle at the top, followed by volume tier cards displayed in a horizontal row. Each tier card shows the tier name, unit price, included volume, overage rate, and a CTA button — the recommended tier gets an accent border and a popular badge. Below the tiers, an interactive usage calculator with a slider lets users estimate their monthly cost. A feature comparison table follows with checkmark/dash cells. The section closes with an FAQ accordion.

responsive.mobile: Tier cards stack vertically in a single column with full-width cards. Usage calculator slider becomes full-width with the cost estimate displayed below. Comparison table converts to a stacked card layout (one card per tier) with feature rows inside each card. FAQ accordion items take full width with larger tap targets.
responsive.tablet: Tier cards display in a 2-column grid, wrapping to a second row if more than two tiers. Comparison table remains tabular but horizontally scrollable if more than three tiers. Calculator and FAQ maintain standard layout.
responsive.desktop: Tier cards display in a single horizontal row (up to four tiers). Comparison table renders as a full-width sticky-header table. Calculator shows slider and cost estimate side-by-side. FAQ renders in a max-width centered column for readability.

composition.PricingUsage: Container(d-section, centered, gap-12) > [BillingToggle(d-control) + TierCards + UsageCalculator? + ComparisonTable? + FAQ?]
composition.TierCard: Card(d-surface, accent-border?: recommended) > [TierName(heading4) + UnitPrice(mono-data, heading2) + Volume + OverageRate(text-muted) + CTAButton(d-interactive)]
